outcome_contract.why: The bar is the screen staff use most every day. Some button colours use redundant opacity syntax that obscures intent, and status indicators use the wrong text colour, making them hard to read. Fixing this means every button, status badge, and price tag looks correct and is easy to read.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All bar/POS components use valid semantic design tokens with correct foreground/background pairing and no redundant opacity values.

engineering_coverage.applicable: All 14 fixes in one pass — complete visual consistency after one commit
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: High fixes first, then incremental improvement

engineering_coverage.applicable: Hover/active and focus ring fixed atomically
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Focus ring fixed in Medium tier, hover/active in Low tier
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: all interaction fixes land together

engineering_coverage.applicable: One contrast sweep verifies all fixes

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B slightly better, but low-risk className changes make this moot

stage_payload.decision_summary: 14 theming issues across 8 bar/POS files need correction. The decision is the implementation strategy: batch all fixes in a single pass vs. split by severity tier. Both approaches use the same target tokens — only execution ordering differs.
goals: Fix 2 High-severity contrast issues (CompScreen status foregrounds)
Clean 5 Medium-severity token/opacity issues (PaymentSection, SalesScreen, HeaderControls)
Clean 7 Low-severity redundant opacity and interaction issues (OrderList, PayModal, Ticket, TicketItems)
non_goals: Redesigning bar layout or component structure
Adding new tokens to the design system
constraints: All changes must use existing semantic tokens — no new token definitions
Purely className adjustments — no component API changes
Preserve intentional opacity on backdrop-blur elements (bg-primary-main/95)
assumptions: Single commit per approach is acceptable (no feature flag needed for className changes)

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Bar is used daily by staff — broken styles disrupt operations
className-only changes are easy to review but many files = larger diff
Must be easy to revert if unexpected

options_considered.Description: Fix all 14 issues in one commit across all 8 files
options_considered.Upside: Fastest delivery; single atomic commit; one review cycle
options_considered.Key risks: If one fix is wrong, the whole commit needs revert or patch

options_considered.Description: Three commits: High first (CompScreen), then Medium, then Low
options_considered.Upside: Incremental delivery; High fixes land first; smaller per-commit diffs
options_considered.Downside: Three review cycles; slightly more git overhead
options_considered.Key risks: Intermediate states have mixed old/new theming

options_considered.Downside: 8 separate commits; excessive overhead for className changes

chosen_approach.Why this wins: All 14 issues are className string replacements with known target values. The risk of any individual fix being wrong is very low (tokens are verified). A single atomic commit delivers full visual consistency immediately and requires only one verification pass. The overhead of tiered commits is not justified for this scope.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: Each fix must be verified against the token resolution chain documented in the fact-find. Intentional opacity on backdrop-blur elements must be preserved.
chosen_approach.Option B (severity-tiered): Unnecessary overhead. Three commits and three verification passes for changes that are all low-risk className replacements. The only benefit (granular revert) is not meaningful when every fix has a verified target token.
chosen_approach.Option C (file-by-file): Over-engineering. 8 commits for className changes is excessive.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (severity-tiered):** Unnecessary overhead. Three commits and three verification passes for changes that are all low-risk className replacements. The only benefit (granular revert) is not meaningful when every fix has a verified target token.
**Option C (file-by-file):** Over-engineering. 8 commits for className changes is excessive.

planning_focus: Single IMPLEMENT task covering all 14 fixes across 8 files
Each fix has a specific old → new className mapping from the fact-find
validation_implications: Visual contrast sweep after commit (both light and dark mode)
Typecheck and lint must pass (className changes don't affect these, but verify)
sequencing_constraints: None — all fixes are independent; no ordering dependency between files
risks_to_carry_forward: HeaderControls `bg-surface/10` replacement needs visual confirmation on primary-main background
